headline: A 2-Year-Old's Gift of Bravery Saves Her Sister's Life
meta_description: When a 5-year-old girl from Salt Lake City needed a bone marrow transplant, her younger sister stepped up with courage beyond her years.
summary_short: Lucy, age 2, became a bone marrow donor for her sister Emma after Emma was diagnosed with aplastic anemia. The successful transplant has given both girls an unbreakable…
body:
A rare bone disease diagnosis just days before Christmas became the catalyst for an extraordinary act of sibling love in Salt Lake City. Five-year-old Emma Meline's mother Amanda noticed a rash and swelling on her daughter's leg, prompting a visit to the pediatrician that would change everything.

The diagnosis was aplastic anemia—a rare condition where bone marrow stops producing blood cells. Emma needed a transplant, and her younger sister Lucy, age 2, turned out to be a perfect genetic match.

Over the following months, as Emma underwent chemotherapy to prepare for the February 17th procedure, little Lucy demonstrated remarkable courage. During blood work appointments, she would tell her mother the same thing: "I'm being brave for Emma."

The operation succeeded completely. By March 11th, mother and daughter returned home to begin recovery. One hundred days after the transplant, Emma showed no recurrence of her disease—a complete success.

What stands out most, Amanda told People, is how Emma now understands what her sister did for her. "She's realizing that Lucy saved her life and they have that bond," she said. "That's so incredible."

Two small sisters, one extraordinary bond forged through medical crisis and childhood bravery.
